description: Default GreedySolver baseline; 29/30 feasible, i16 infeasible

constrained_first init restored + rescue skip; -7.9% avg_cost vs exp003; 29/30 feasible
LAHC(L=100)+blocking destroy; +0.47% regression vs exp004; i16 2->1 viol but still infeasible
surgeon-aware blocking destroy (no LAHC); +0.28% regression vs exp004; i16 still 1 viol; i20 +6.8%
rescue-gate(N=50)+blocking destroy; -1.9% avg_cost vs exp004; i16 first feasible; 30/30 feasible
adaptive destroy [15-40%->10-30%]; +2.6% regression vs exp007; i16 infeasible again; rescue-gate breaks
perturbation restart(N=500,50%); +1.9% regression vs exp007; i16 infeasible; disrupts rescue-gate
feasibility-gated perturb(N=500,50%); +0.056% vs exp007; 30/30 feasible; N=500 too infrequent
feasibility-gated perturb(N=100,50%); -0.044% vs exp007; 30/30 feasible; new best
4-worker parallel LNS + 600s time limit (competition-legal baseline); 30/30 feasible
perturb_ratio 0.30 + weighted ops [1,2,2] + room-proximity related; 29/30 feasible; i16 infeasible again
SA acceptance (T0=2%*init_cost, alpha=0.9997); 29/30 feasible; 2.4% worse than exp011; SA too hot at 60s
regret-2 repair (static pre-sort by second_best−best); best 4-worker/60s so far; 29/30 feasible
ALNS op-weights (sigma1=33, decay=0.8, seg=100) + regret; −0.2% vs exp015; 29/30 feasible
regret+ALNS at 1-worker/60s; 29/30 feasible; 2% worse than exp011; regret halves iteration count
ALNS alone (no regret), 1-worker/60s; 29/30 feasible; 2.3% worse than exp011; diversity reduction hurts
high_delay perturb restart; 29/30 feasible; 2.7% worse than exp011; deterministic targeting reduces diversity
window-sort repair (narrowest window first); 29/30 feasible; 2.6% worse than exp011; disrupts temporal spreading
N=50/ratio=0.40 perturbation; 29/30 feasible; 2.5% worse than exp011; too frequent restarts hurt
heavy_day 4th op (25% each); 29/30 feasible; 2.6% worse; dilutes existing 3 operators
larger-destroy (15-35%); 29/30 feasible; 3.2% worse than exp011; fewer iters outweigh larger moves
smaller-destroy (5-20%); 29/30 feasible; best since exp011; more iters with finer moves
tiny-destroy (3-12%); 29/30 feasible; best yet; more iters > stronger moves
micro-destroy (1-6%); 29/30; 0.57% from exp011; trend continues
nano-destroy (0.5-2%); 28/30 feasible; too small k; diversity collapse
less-perturb (N=300 vs 100); 29/30 feasible; identical to exp026; perturbation freq not binding
multi-worker (4 workers); 29/30 feasible; 0.23% from exp011; seed diversity helps
